items.headline: Seeds of Peace Monument agreement approved for Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust regarding the Seeds of Peace Monument, an artwork by artist Mario Chiodo to be located at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. This public art installation adds a new memorial feature to the park.

items.headline: Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District budget approved
items.summary: Council took action on the Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District's 2026-2027 budget report and annual assessment. The BID finances improvements and services within that commercial corridor.

items.headline: Cupertino updates Health and Safety Element of General Plan
items.summary: The City Council reviewed updates to the Health and Safety Element of the Cupertino General Plan covering 2015–2040. This element guides how the city addresses public health, emergency preparedness, and safety infrastructure in future development and policy decisions.

items.headline: City Council Appoints New Permanent City Manager
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution appointing a permanent City Manager. This fills a leadership position responsible for overseeing day-to-day city operations and implementing council policies.

items.headline: Urgency Ordinance and New Municipal Code Section Introduced
items.summary: The Council conducted a public hearing and considered adopting an urgency ordinance adding a new section to the municipal code, as well as introducing a related ordinance. The urgency designation suggests the measure addresses a time-sensitive issue requiring immediate action.

items.headline: Site Development Permit Amendment and Tentative Map Approved
items.summary: The Council adopted a resolution approving a Site Development Permit Amendment and Vesting Tentative Map, indicating a development project modification was finalized.

items.headline: Sunnyvale updates urban water master plan amid shortage risks
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission will review the draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. This plan addresses how the city manages water supply and responds to drought conditions.

items.headline: Spring community grants available for local events and projects
items.summary: The City Council will consider 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grant Program applications in the spring cycle on May 18. Residents and organizations can apply for funding to support local events and neighborhood initiatives.
